texto: The ultimate computer game would be a 'total addiction' - a game that shapes itself to the elements you most like to play in such a manner that it totally satisfies you. You never want to stop playing. However, any self-configuring activity always has such risks inherent in it. Will we see one day the goverment insisting that games have time limiters the way that some motor vehicles have speed limiters?
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Ian Pearson: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “The ultimate computer game would be a 'total addiction' - a game that shapes itself to the elemen…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que autoestima, addiction, computer-games pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de computer-games e future.
